After practicing their French skills on the street, the next stop was thevintage store Metallic Curtain, where they browse the racks and discuss everything from Balenciaga to Kurt Cobain to the growing popularity of vintage shopping in Korea. posts.caption: The  new It-shoe is the skimpy little sandal. Not only was it spotted on the Celine men’s spring 2027 runway, but also on the exposed toes of some show-goers during men’s fashion week in Paris. In addition to the Literary Sport collaboration, Xero—whose popular Genesis sandal features just a 5.5 mm (0.2 inch) rubber sole and corded rope fastenings—recently partnered with J.Crew on versions with specialty rope colors or a gingham-printed footbed. Vogue Fashion Fund finalist Jamie Haller, known for her handsome loafers, has jettisoned all the bulk for summer, too, with her aptly named String Theory sandal. Learn more about the silhouette and shop the shoe at the link in bio.

posts.alt_text: Can a mere mortal get in Greek god shape by following Matt Damon's workout plan? With the help of physical therapist and performance coach Dr. Gabe Stump, whom Damon has worked with since 2022, the 55-year-old Academy Award-winning actor had to train hard and eat right to maintain the body of a hero worthy of a generation-spanning epic tale. At the link in bio, one Vogue writer dives deep into the star's blockbuster workout routine—and does a lot of reps—in hopes of achieving a statuesque physique like Odysseus.

posts.caption: What goes around comes around, and Bermuda shorts are back! Arguably, this is the summer of the silhouette. Discover and shop five contemporary ways to style the Bermuda short for summer at the link in bio.

posts.alt_text:
Melanie Chisolm (@melaniecmusic) did not grow up fantasizing about her wedding day. Known as "Sporty Spice", being one fifth of the biggest girl group of all time was top of mind instead for Mel C. “I’d gotten to the point in my life where I just didn’t think it was going to be a part of my story,” she says now. Then, along came @ChrisDingwall. Chris popped the question last July, and the couple set about planning a relaxed summer celebration at a friend’s lakeside property in Cumbria, preceded by a legal ceremony in the groom’s native Australia. 

While Melanie wasn't particularly interested in a traditional wedding, she did end up observing one major bridal custom: a "something borrowed." Her dress for the Sydney ceremony came from the wardrobe of none other than her friend and fellow Spice Girl, fashion designer @VictoriaBeckham. It didn't stop there, for their larger celebration, Victoria offered to make Melanie her dream dress. “Victoria wanted to make it much more personal to me," the bride explains. Tap the link in our bio for a look inside the couple's celebration.

Photos: @timmsy

posts.caption: “I am not a sweatpants person, but I like to be comfortable,” says Melissa Morris (@melissa.metier), founder of @Métier. “You can still look put together while feeling relaxed!” It’s an ethos that extends to her luxury leather accessories label. Polished structure and rich materials rule—but the pieces are designed to be lived with. Naturally, her travel capsule wardrobe reflects this approach, too. Everything is well-made, high-quality, and designed to last; but with comfort as the priority, she doesn’t have to be too precious with anything. Discover now the luxury accessories designer makes the most out of her travel wardrobe at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Having the courage to step into the unknown—that is the commanding trait of our 'Audacity' issue. It is something our cover star @chua.tanya has never shied away from, even at the most tumultuous point of her career.
With little to no support for her musical direction back then, the esteemed Singaporean singer-songwriter was determined to escape to Taiwan with only three months worth of rent in her bank account. "I had just won a Golden Melody Award, but it’s not like that helps pay the bills. I let myself disintegrate entirely to find myself again. It was a turning point for me because it allowed me to ponder this question of why we are so afraid of failure," she reminisces.
For #VogueSingapore's July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, Chua lays herself bare, sharing with us the mettle behind her musical vision, her boundless curiosity for life and trying out new things. Read the full story now in the link in bio and preorder a copy, out on newsstands on 22 July.
Editor-in-chief: @monkiepoo
Photography: @chou_mo_ | @overface_artists
Stylist: @mystrangeanarchy
Producer & videographer: @davidbay
Photo assistant: Yen, Lu Shao Cheng
Hair stylist: @mikioaiz | @overface_artists
Make-up: @kaiting_w
Manicure: @iconnail
Set design: @shuoliang
Set supervisor: @naoto_doji
Set assistant: @itsmrcole
Floral design: @temperature_official
Production: @overface_production
Jewellery: @tiffanyandco
Talent: @chua.tanya
Story: @ehnyway
posts.alt_text:
Curiousity never killed the cat. So at 51, @chua.tanya is still doing hardcore gymnastics; an indisputable reflection of what has always been a lifelong resolve—to always try the things that scare you. 

"I’m doing backflips and handstands at an age where most people would be terrified to try. But if you’re constantly fearful of failure, nothing is going to get done. You’ll never do anything interesting or out of the ordinary," our 'Audacity' cover star proclaims. 

In the link in bio, the Singaporean singer-songwriter's narrative may read mercurial yet there was always a steadfast nerve behind it all: be it back when she debuted with her English album in 1997, when she wanted to do a tour dedicated to her less recognised tracks or even when she decided to take up gymnastics later in life. 

The July/Aug 'Audacity' issue is available for pre-order, and will hit newsstands on 22 July. 

Editor-in-chief: @monkiepoo

posts.caption:
The streets of Seoul; a cacophony of sounds; a smorgasbord of sights. Earlier this year, the South Korean capital played out as the vibrant locale in which @chanelofficial hosted its replica show for its Métiers d'art 2026—one which @shiiiannn sat front row at. The emerging starlet and #VogueSingapore's digital cover star for July/August has been on the up and up, with roles spanning 'Amoeba', 'We Learn to Breathe in Distant Places' and 'Secondary: The Musical'.
With performances marked by a rare duality—earnest yet incisive, tender yet unflinching—she brings a striking depth to every character she inhabits. Similarly, for her time in Seoul, she brings her introspective nature to various locations that feel intrinsically part of the city's cultural fabric, as #VogueSingapore lenses her in the chaos of a pocha, or in the company of Buchaechum dancers.
In the link in bio and our July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, the Singaporean darling shares more about her craft, dream projects and ties to the fashion world.

posts.caption:
What did image-making constitute before the camera? The two-century old procedure of lumen printing; the development of the Polaroid in the ‘40s; the experience of darkroom photography. Image-making as a practice is an art in of itself—a form which is explored in an experimental spread for #VogueSingapore’s ‘Nomad’ issue. Now out on newsstands, take a swirl in the magic of these image-making techniques of the past, from plays on shadow and light to the vivid imprints left behind on photographic paper that is sensitive to the sun.

posts.caption: As far as Chinese actors go, @rooosyzh09's name is second to none. A beloved figure for her daring, lively and feminine spirit, #ZhaoLusi's influence makes her the perfect match for @chowtaifookjewellery, the leading Chinese jewellery house that consistently draws from traditional aesthetics—adapting it for a modern audience. In the latest campaign, Zhao is captured in the Dawn collection, which reimagines time-honoured motifs, as striking red enamel finds contemporary flavour in confident, elegant details. #VogueSingapore
posts.alt_text:
Having the courage to step into the unknown—that is the commanding trait of our 'Audacity' issue. It is something our cover star @chua.tanya has never shied away from, even at the most tumultuous point of her career.

With little to no support for her musical direction back then, the esteemed Singaporean singer-songwriter was determined to escape to Taiwan with only three months worth of rent in her bank account. "I had just won a Golden Melody Award, but it’s not like that helps pay the bills. I let myself disintegrate entirely to find myself again. It was a turning point for me because it allowed me to ponder this question of why we are so afraid of failure," she reminisces. 

For #VogueSingapore's July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, Chua lays herself bare, sharing with us the mettle behind her musical vision, her boundless curiosity for life and trying out new things. Read the full story now in the link in bio and preorder a copy, out on newsstands on 22 July. 

Editor-in-chief: @monkiepoo
Photography: @chou_mo_ | @overface_artists
Stylist: @mystrangeanarchy
Producer & videographer: @davidbay

posts.caption: Of course, there had to be an array of celebs at the @fifaworldcup—@lamineyamal’s little brother Keyne included. Stars flocked in to watch the culmination of an exhilarating tournament; from @eileengu supporting @sefutbol, to the Beckham clan rooting for the Three Lions all the way to bronze. @tomcruise also made a surprise speech for the closing ceremony, while @hoooooyeony and @carlitosalcarazz helped unveil the @louisvuitton trophy trunk. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
: ❤️🌍 Distance may separate two people, but the right habits can keep love stronger than ever.
From meaningful conversations and virtual date nights to trust, patience, and planning your next reunion, it's the little things that help long-distance relationships thrive. Love isn't measured in miles—it's measured in effort. ✨
💬 What's one habit that has helped your long-distance relationship stay strong? Share your experience in the comments! 👇
💾 Save this post for the days you miss your person, and tag someone who's making long-distance love work. ❤️
📍Follow @weddingaffairofficial for relationship advice, modern love stories, and wedding inspiration.
#longdistancerelationships #ldr #relationshipadvice #healthyrelationship #modernlove
posts.alt_text:
: Love isn’t kept alive by grand gestures alone… it’s the little things that matter most 🤍✨

Late-night conversations, random hugs, coffee dates, checking in during busy days, holding hands, and never stopping the effort — these small habits keep romance alive long after the wedding day 💫

Because the strongest marriages are built on everyday love 🌸

posts.alt_text:
KAVITAON KA SILSILA AA RAHA HAI @sohotheatre LONDON. 💌🫀

They say everything iconic has once happened at SOHO (@sohotheatre ). The thought that Rajkumari will now be counted among the stories told on that stage still doesn’t feel real.

It’s strange… I can often find words for emotions people have buried deep within them, but when it comes to this show~my own, I always fall short.

Rajkumari has changed my life in ways I don’t think I’ll ever fully be able to put into poetry.

The last time we were in London, we promised we’d return bigger and better.

We kept that promise.

This is our biggest London show yet, and I’d love for you to be in the room with us, to laugh, cry, heal, and become a part of this chapter.

Tickets are in my bio. See you at Soho. 🔗🤍

@tarashna.management @beacon.live

posts.caption:
✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
Still trying to reply to every DM, every message, every blessing… and smiling through all of them. 🥹❤️
The love you’ll have given me & Rohan has been unreal. Thank you for celebrating us like we’re your own family.
And oh my God… the love for our outfits! 🥹 Every message appreciating the tiniest personalized details, every “I noticed this,” every compliment… it truly feels like you guys see us. You saw the thought, the emotion, the intention behind every little choice we made. That means more to us than you’ll ever know. Thank you. ❤️
Everyone’s asking us the same questions—
“How did this happen?”
“How did you get married there?”
“How did you even dream of this?”
The truth is… we don’t have an answer.
Some dreams aren’t planned. They’re blessed. 🤍🕉️
If there’s one person who made all of this possible, it’s Mahadev. We were just lucky enough to be chosen.
From the bottom of our hearts… thank you for every prayer, every tear, every kind word, and every bit of love you’ve sent our way. We don’t take a single bit of it for granted.
And… ये तो सिर्फ ट्रेलर था… पिक्चर अभी बाकी है मेरे दोस्त. ❤️✨
Bride in : @papadontpreachbyshubhika
Groom in: @jigarandnikitaofficial
Makeup: @sugandhkhannamakeup

Events: @kkingsevents
Design: @konarkweddings

posts.caption:
Rahul Mishra @rahulmishra_7 presented ‘Devi’ at Haute Couture Week, Paris. Drawing inspiration from ancient South Indian sculptures and the divine feminine, the collection was an absolute showstopper.
The lineup effortlessly transformed featherlight fabrics into breathtaking, structured silhouettes. By blending traditional artistry like intricate hand embroidery, zardozi, and dabka with modern design; Mishra beautifully celebrated India's rich artisanal heritage. The collection's contemporary edge was further elevated through brilliant collaborations with clay artisan Sumant Kumar, legendary milliner Stephen Jones, and Tanishq Natural Diamonds, seamlessly breathing new life into centuries-old craftsmanship.
Jewellery by @tanishqjewellery
Visit www.weddingvows.com for latest updates in wedding trends, region based vendors and bridal inspiration.
#weddingvowsmagazine #cardiB #ishaambani #rahulmishra #taylor

posts.caption:
"Listen. I’m not going to be mad at anyone calling me beautiful,” @lupitanyongo says. In Director Christopher Nolan's much-anticipated, much-shrouded blockbuster adaptation of The Odyssey, Nyong'o plays Helen of Troy, the mythic face that "launched a thousand ships." When asked how it sits with her to be playing one of the most beautiful women of all time, Nyong'o responds enthusiastically, "Really well!" While many women struggle to accept a single compliment, the Oscar-winning actress is fully owning her turn as the legendary Greek figure. After all, regal, striking beauty is inextricable from Nyong'o's public identity: as Nakia, the warrior/spy and love of the late Chadwick Boseman's King T'Challa in Black Panther; as the first Black face of Lancôme; and as a Chanel ambassador. But what fans may not realize is that Nyong'o is pulling double duty in the film by portraying Helen's sister Clytemnestra too.
In our July cover story, @michandrews speaks to Nyong'o about starring in the cultural event of the summer, letting her work speak for itself, and how her ongoing battle with uterine fibroids sparked the Make Fibroids Count research campaign. Read the full interview at the link in our bio.
Photographer / Creative Conception: @knngrm
Stylist: @jan.quammie
Creative Direction: @bonatalia
Hair Stylist: @vernonfrancois
Hair Stylist Assistant: @batiseolhair
Makeup Artist: @nickbarose using @chanel.beauty
Makeup Assistant: @golfxmua
Manicurist: Bagir Hasanov
Set Designer: @lane.vineyard
Editor in Chief: @katcollings
Entertainment Director: @jbake21
Writer: @michandrews

posts.caption:
A weekend in Copenhagen with @lirisaw. We got a firsthand look inside the first-ever pop-up for Law’s Japanese tea brand, LITTLE SIP. The passion project is deeply personal for Law, who worked closely with tea farmers in Kawane, Japan to source each tea for the special moment at @tambo.kitchen in Copenhagen. Ahead, Law shares a recap of the event with us.
my first little sip tea pop up at tambo kitchen in Copenhagen 🍵
mode of transport gathering groceries the day before
four japanese teas were available all sourced from different farmers i met in kawane 🫖🫖🫖🫖
glowing iced sencha with @bernardocostantino’s sweet pea bites
my friend tim from postcard teas inspired this way of making sparkling tea
🐥 in uniform
💞🫛
tea~ware by franca christopherson commissioned specially to serve first and second infusion of sencha together
~*

posts.caption:
There will be many high-stakes returns at the spring 2027 edition of @londonfashionweek this September as the @britishfashioncouncil on Monday unveiled the provisional schedule for the five-day fashion showcase slated for Sept. 17 to 21.⁠
⁠
@alexandermcqueen is back on home turf from Paris, with @⁠
sean.mcgirr's London debut presenting coed men’s and women’s spring 2027 collections on Sept. 20.⁠
⁠
The brand’s newly appointed chief executive officer Gianfranco D’Attis told WWD earlier that “bringing McQueen back to London Fashion Week is a meaningful moment for the house. London is where our story began and remains central to our identity.”⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @tianweizhang⁠
Photo: @giovanni_giannoni_photo/WWD
posts.alt_text:
The first FIFA World Cup final halftime show had 11 minutes, more than 350 performers and apparently no interest in settling on one dress code. Madonna opened in pink Saint Laurent and corsetry, BTS arrived as a seven-man team in red, white and black, Justin Bieber played acoustic guitar in custom Skylrk and Shakira took the field barefoot in enough fringe to register from the upper deck.⁠
⁠
Madonna made her entrance through the stadium tunnels in a dune buggy driven by Brazilian football legends Ronaldo and Ronaldinho, then opened the show with a new version of “Music” incorporating elements of “Disco Inferno” and “Danceteria.” The two players flanked her in Brazil-colored tracksuits, one primarily yellow and the other blue, while Madonna went in a completely different direction.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: Maggie Clancy

posts.caption:
Jennifer Hudson embraced the spotlight in Thom Browne during the 2026 World Cup Final on Sunday at New York New Jersey Stadium, where she performed the U.S. national anthem.
Hudson wore a custom three-piece Thom Browne look in white, with navy piping and the designer’s signature red, white and navy stripes providing the color. Her fitted silk crepe jacket featured structured shoulders, a single-button waist and a rounded hem, with the striped band circling the upper left sleeve. She paired the crisp blazer with a square-neck corset and matching floor-length pencil skirt.
Learn more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: Julia Teti, Maggie Clancy

posts.caption:
This summer’s sports fervor has hit an all time high. From the New York Knicks’ historic basketball win to Formula 1 Racing, the Wimbledon championships and the FIFA World Cup, wearing your favorite team’s jersey has become a valid fashion statement. But fashion and sport actually have a long and storied relationship, one that continues to show up on the global runway.⁠
⁠
One of the first designers to bring the kitsch of it all to the runways was American designer Geoffrey Beene, whose 1967 sequined sheath inspired by the football jersey, brought sports style to the high street. Also in the mix as early as the 1900s was Lacoste, known for bringing fashionable tennis style to the streets. Taking a cue from Beene, Tom Ford reimagined the iconic 1967 sequined football jersey dress for his Tom Ford fall 2014 collection. While Ralph Lauren and Tommy Hilfiger continue to keep sports-lifestyle branding in their yearly collections.⁠
⁠
Today, sports culture feels omnipresent on the runway as streetwear continues to trend on the high street, giving athletes and fans a fashionable way to stay connected to their favorite sports through designer looks. So whether you’re a fan or fanatic, everyone is on the fun.⁠
⁠
See more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @tonymbl⁠
Photos: Fairchild Archive/WWD, John Aquino/Fairchild Archive, @kate_jones_photo/WWD, Fairchild Archive/WWD, @giovanni_giannoni_photo/Fairchild Archive, @simonelezzi/WWD, WWD⁠
⁠
1. Football at Tom Ford, fall 2014. ⁠
2. Baseball at DKNY worn by Josie Borain, 1993. ⁠
3. Basketball at Gypsy Sport, spring 2022.⁠
4. Golf at Ralph Lauren, Spring 1990. ⁠
5. Football at Alexander McQueen, spring 2005. ⁠
6. Motor racing from Gigi Hadid x Tommy Hilfiger, 2018. ⁠
7. Off-White's "Track and Field" spring 2019. ⁠
8. Motorcross at Fenty x Puma spring 2018, worn by Adriana Lima.⁠
9. Tennis whites at Lacoste, spring 2011. ⁠
10. Soccer at Louis Vuitton, spring 2025.

posts.caption:
Ahead of his highly anticipated @fifaworldcup halftime performance on Sunday, @lilbieber took over New York City with two immersive pop-ups for his design-led fashion and consumer technology brand @skylrk.
On Thursday, Skylrk opened a pop-up shop in New York City at 60 Tenth Avenue, running through Sunday. The brand noted lines started forming on Wednesday night and were wrapped around the block throughout the first two opening days. Inside the space, the immersive environment featured sculptural fixtures with interactive shopping vignettes alongside LED screen-filled walls and a large geodesic-inspired dome installation.
The temporary space, powered by Cash App, is the brand’s third physical retail appearance, and first in New York City, following a November event in Tokyo and Bieber’s Skylrk Oasis during Coachella in Indio, Calif., earlier this year.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report/Video: @elmercer
